title: Cross-Corridor Payment Cost Comparison — Sending Money to China
methodology: Each corridor researched across 7 cost dimensions (FX markup, transfer fees, intermediary fees, account costs, speed, safety/regulation, limitations). Bank costs use standard retail/SME online banking rates. WorldFirst costs use maximum published FX ceilings per corridor from the WorldFirst Product Handbook. All costs estimated on a $10,000 USD equivalent transfer.

corridors.unique_finding: FX markup constitutes 85–95% of true bank transfer cost. Santander only bank publishing tiered markup.
Australian banks charge 2–4x more for CNY than USD. NAB: 2.54% USD vs 10.4% CNY. Westpac blocks personal CNY.
Most expensive corridor. Double conversion structural (TRY→USD→CNY). Thinnest fintech market — most global fintechs don't serve Turkey outbound.
Fintech desert for outbound. Wise cannot send from VND. $254B bilateral trade. Only major ASEAN economy without a PBOC swap.
Emerging Africa-China fintechs (Pesa, Klasha, Importa Pay) beat WF on price. Multi-rate FX system. Form M mandatory. Record $24.9B Chinese exports.
World Bank reports zero qualifying outbound services. RMB share tripled to 19.1%. CPEC driving adoption. Hawala is real competitor.
China reclaimed Germany's #1 partner (€251.8B). Deutsche Bank charges 1.5% of amount. Frankfurt RMB clearing. EUR/CNY CFETS weight 17.9%.
Thinnest data corridor. LC mandatory. World's 2nd largest garment exporter. Zero fintechs serve outbound. No BDT-CNY interbank market.
BNP most competitive French bank. Banque Populaire integrates Wise natively. Paris RMB clearing center. Crédit Agricole 0.05% commission is misleading.

corridors.wf_advantage: ING beats WF on headline price
corridors.unique_finding: ING's 0.85% koersopslag is the most competitive traditional bank rate found across all 15 corridors. EU's largest China deficit (€72.4B). Rotterdam = Europe's China gateway.

corridors.wf_advantage: Marginal vs banks, not cheapest overall
corridors.unique_finding: AED pegged to USD removes first FX leg. Dragon Mart + 8,000 Chinese firms. Exchange houses beat WF on personal. WF value is B2B.
SAR pegged to USD. Petro-yuan developing but commercial adoption near zero. Vision 2030 fintech push. Narrowest WF price advantage of any corridor.
$153B trade. PBOC-BOT swap CNY 70B. ICBC Thai clearing bank. PromptPay-China QR (Oct 2025). Wise launching THB outbound May 2026.
$135B trade. PBOC-BI swap CNY 400B (one of largest). LCS/LCT framework live. Not a fintech desert — Flip, Transfez compete.
$155.6B trade (record), $116B deficit. No RBI-PBOC swap (political tensions). WF P1 pricing too high vs Indian banks. TCS 5% on remittances.

rankings.largest_wf_advantage: Australia (13–15x on CNY transfers — banks charge 8.8–10.4% for CNY)
rankings.thinnest_data: Bangladesh (LC mandatory, zero fintechs, World Bank has no data)
rankings.thinnest_fintech_market: Turkey (most global fintechs don't serve outbound)
rankings.most_competitive_bank: ING Netherlands (0.85% koersopslag — lowest bank markup across all 15 corridors)
rankings.largest_bilateral_trade: Vietnam ($254B), followed by Indonesia ($135B) and India ($155.6B)

geo_cross_corridor_paragraph: The cost of sending $10,000 to China varies dramatically by origin country. Turkey is the most expensive corridor, with banks averaging approximately 10.4% in total transfer costs due to a structural double conversion (TRY to USD to CNY). Australian banks charge 8.8–10.4% specifically for CNY transfers — 2 to 4 times their own USD rates. UK banks cost approximately £255–410 per £10,000, while German banks cost approximately €300–700. At the lower end, French and Dutch banks offer approximately €150–509 depending on the institution, with ING Netherlands at 0.85% being the most competitive traditional bank found across all 15 corridors researched. WorldFirst's cost ranges from approximately 0.5% (UK) to 2.25% (UAE/Saudi Arabia), depending on the corridor's pricing tier. In four corridors — Nigeria, Bangladesh, India, and the Netherlands — WorldFirst is not the cheapest option. This research covers 15 origin countries and approximately 80 providers, with data sourced from bank fee schedules, independent comparison platforms (Wise, Monito, Exiap, KeyCurrency), central bank reference rates, and regulatory filings. Data collected April 2026.

compliance_notes.disclaimer: Cost estimates based on published rates and independent comparison data as of April 2026. Actual costs vary by provider, account type, relationship, transfer amount, and market conditions. WorldFirst costs use maximum published FX ceilings per corridor. This comparison is for illustrative purposes and does not constitute financial advice.
compliance_notes.methodology: Bank costs derived from published fee schedules, third-party comparison data, and independent rate sampling. WorldFirst costs use maximum published rates from the WorldFirst Product Handbook, not typical or negotiated rates. All comparative claims include 'approximately' or 'estimated' qualifiers.
